channel.title: _metapages on James Akl
channel.description: Recent content in _metapages on James Akl

item.title: About
item.description: I am a roboticist with a background in engineering, mathematics, and science. Currently, I am a Doctoral Researcher at Worcester Polytechnic Institute working in the Manipulation & Environmental Robotics Lab. You can reach me at james-akl@outlook.com. Interests My main interests are in Autonomous & Intelligent Systems. For this, I focus on the relevant advances, sciences, and practices within: Robotics & Automation Artificial Intelligence Control & Dynamical Systems Mathematics & Formal Systems Software & Computing Systems Mechatronic Systems I also find biologically-driven fields (e.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Some of my personal projects are showcased below. [[View tags](/ptags)] -- Convex Analysis & Optimization: Select Works Collection of interesting problems with my proofs and solutions. Numerical Analysis & Implementation: 1D PDE [GitHub] Numerical linear algebraic solution of a time–dependent wave PDE. Finite Element Analysis & Implementation: 1D PDE [PDF][GitHub] Designing and optimizing a finite element solution to a 1D PDE. Inverse Problems for Disturbance Observer Control [PDF][GitHub] Regularization and optimization applied to DOB control.

item.title: Research
item.description: Some of my research contributions are disclosed below. Publications [PDF][Dataset] ZeroWaste Dataset: Towards Deformable Object Segmentation in Extreme Clutter, 2022 IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR). Dina Bashkirova; Mohamed Abdelfattah; Ziliang Zhu; James Akl; Fadi Alladkani; Ping Hu; Vitaly Ablavsky; Berk Calli; Sarah Adel Bargal; Kate Saenko. [PDF][Video] Towards Robotic Metal Scrap Cutting: A Novel Workflow and Pipeline for Cutting Path Generation, 2021 IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE).

item.title: Resume
item.description: My professional and educational backgrounds are shared below. View my resume View my coursework I value openness and transparency, but also confidentiality. For private information, please request directly at james-akl@outlook.com.

item.title: Writings
item.description: Some of my personal writings are listed below. [[View tags](/tags)] -- Reading Spatial Transforms Different ways to interpret a homogeneous (rigid-body) transform. A Tour of Robot Mechanics Broad but rigorous glide over robot kinematics and dynamics. Defining a Robot An evolving attempt to answer “What is a robot?” in a prescriptive manner.
